display: NIH SBIR Phase I (Small Business Innovation Research)
_provenance: Structure reflects NIH SBIR/STTR Phase I (SF424 R&R) conventions. Phase I funding ceilings, page limits, and eligibility MUST be verified against the specific NOFO and the current SBIR/STTR program solicitation; agency-specific budget caps differ and change yearly.

research_strategy._note: NIH SBIR Phase I Research Strategy is typically shorter than R01; confirm exact limit in the NOFO.

section_intent.specific_aims: Feasibility-focused aims for a short Phase I; clear go/no-go milestones.
section_intent.significance: Commercial and scientific problem; market/clinical need; why this is a small-business opportunity.
section_intent.innovation: Technical innovation and competitive differentiation vs existing products and prior SBIR/STTR awards.
section_intent.approach: Phase I feasibility plan with quantitative success criteria and milestones; risk mitigation.
section_intent.commercialization_potential: Target market, customers, competition, IP/FTO posture, path to revenue, and what Phase II would fund.

budget_rules._cap_note: NIH follows SBA-set guideline ceilings for Phase I and may grant exceptions for specific topics; the exact dollar figure changes and is topic-dependent. VERIFY against the current solicitation.

indirect._note: SBIR indirect/F&A handling differs from R01; small businesses without a negotiated rate may use a provisional rate per the solicitation.

fee_profit._note: SBIR allows a fixed fee (profit) — unusual vs research grants; verify allowable percentage.

budget_rules.ai_policy: AI MUST NOT invent dollar amounts, caps, fee percentages, or rates. All numeric values come from the applicant. AI may only (a) check completeness, (b) flag suspected cap/category/fee violations as 'verify against solicitation', and (c) draft justification narrative from applicant-provided numbers.

review_criteria._source: NIH SBIR/STTR peer review (verify against current solicitation).
review_criteria.note: Commercialization potential is weighted for SBIR and is absent from a standard R01.

reviewer_personas.focus: Technical feasibility: success criteria, milestones, controls, statistics for a short Phase I.
Technical and commercial differentiation vs existing products and prior SBIR/STTR awards; FTO posture.
Completeness/compliance: SBIR eligibility, required sections, page limits, budget+fee justification, commercialization plan.
